title: Multi-Channel Expansion: eBay, OnBuy & Temu Integration
summary: Unified catalogue, pricing, and inventory across eBay, OnBuy, and Temu while maintaining Amazon as the anchor channel — adding £380K incremental annual revenue without operational breakdown.
challenge: Revenue was over-concentrated on Amazon (78% of total GMV), creating platform risk. eBay and OnBuy had legacy listings with poor imagery and no promoted listing strategy. Temu represented an untapped volume opportunity but required careful margin modelling.
strategy: Built a channel-tier pricing framework: Amazon as premium anchor, eBay/OnBuy as reach channels with promoted visibility, and Temu as volume driver for selected margin-positive SKUs. Centralised inventory sync rules to prevent overselling during peak periods.
implementation: Migrated 340 listings to OnBuy with unified imagery, titles, and category mapping
Launched eBay Promoted Listings campaigns with ROAS targets per category
Structured Temu catalogue with 95 margin-positive SKUs and automated repricing floors
Implemented cross-channel inventory sync with safety stock buffers per platform
Created unified weekly P&L reporting across all 6 channels in Looker Studio
